_meta.title: FF Load
_meta.note: video is patched with an annotated path: '<subfolder>/<file>.mp4 [output]' to read from the ComfyUI output dir

_meta.title: FF Upscale
_meta.note: VALIDATED live 2026-07-14 against 192.168.4.196:8188/object_info/SeedVR2VideoUpscaler: image/dit/vae links; seed INT 0-4294967295; resolution INT (shortest edge, step 2); max_resolution INT (0 = no cap); batch_size INT min 1 step 4 (the 4n+1 grid — 81 legal); uniform_batch_size BOOLEAN; color_correction enum [lab, wavelet, wavelet_adaptive, hsv, adain, none]; optional temporal_overlap 0-16 / prepend_frames 0-32 INT, input_noise_scale/latent_noise_scale FLOAT 0-1, offload_device enum [none, cpu, cuda:0] default cpu. DiT model seedvr2_ema_7b_sharp_fp8_e4m3fn.safetensors and VAE ema_vae_fp16.safetensors both confirmed in the live loader enums; both AUTO-DOWNLOAD on first use (loader tooltip) — first hero run blocks on ~9GB of downloads. FRAME MATH: RIFE'd Wan finals are ~161 frames (81 -> mult-2 -> 161), so batch 81 means batches [81, 80] with a possible temporal seam at the boundary (temporal_overlap 0); only raw 16fps Wan clips (81f) fit one batch. VRAM (32GB RTX PRO 4500): batch 81 @ 1080p is UNPROVEN — controls are sane (upscaler offload_device cpu; DiT fp8 ~8GB fits without blockswap) but if the first hero run OOMs the knobs are, in order: batch_size 21, DiT loader blocks_to_swap >0 + offload_device cpu, VAE decode_tiled true.

_meta.title: FF Output
_meta.note: frame_rate and filename_prefix are BOTH patched per job by /api/finish (frame_rate must match the input clip's fps — 32 for RIFE'd Wan finals, 16 raw Wan, 24 LTX Flash).
